title: Malaxis autumnalis
intro: Malaxis autumnalis, also known as the autumn lady's-tresses, is a species of orchid native to North America, characterized by its slender stems and clusters of small, greenish-yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Malaxis autumnalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat coughs, fever, and other respiratory conditions.
taxonomy: Malaxis autumnalis is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Malaxis and is often referred to as the autumn-spurred orchid. This classification is based on its unique characteristics and features. Malaxis autumnalis is a terrestrial orchid native to North America and is often found in woodland areas and along streams. The classification of Malaxis autumnalis is subject to ongoing research and possible revisions in the field of botany.
uses: Malaxis autumnalis, also known as the autumnal adder's mouth or autumn fairy orchid, is a species of orchid that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
